solution.p: First we arrange so the radical term is the only term on one side.
Now we square each side to obtain a quadratic equation. Then we can rearrange that into standard form.
Now we can identify a={4}\text{,} b={-42}\text{,} and c={54}\text{,} and apply the quadratic formula.
The only possible solutions are {{\frac{3}{2}}} and {9}\text{.} They each check as valid solutions in the original equation, so the solution set is {\left\{\frac{3}{2},9\right\}}\text{.}
